items.title: Idea-to-build: A Claude brainstormer that critiques your idea, then builds it
items.why_it_matters: An open-source tool that acts as a sparring partner for new project ideas, pushing back on weak points before scaffolding the code. Since you are looking to ship your next revenue project, you can run this tool locally this week to stress-test and generate the boilerplate for your next micro-SaaS idea.

items.title: AI Gauge: Track your Claude and Codex usage limits in one desktop app
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer built a desktop monitor to track API and session usage limits across Claude and Codex, built entirely using agentic coding. You can clone the repo today to see how they structured their desktop wrapper, and run it to avoid getting locked out of Claude mid-build.

items.title: Trending 'skills' repos for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Developers are sharing custom 'skills'—bash commands and automated workflows—to extend Claude Code's capabilities. You can look at these trending repos today and write your first custom skill to automate git commits or database migrations in your AI-ingest project.

items.title: AgentLoop: A minimal, readable Claude agent starter kit
items.why_it_matters: A lightweight, open-source starter kit designed to let you build Claude-powered agents without the bloat of massive frameworks. You can clone this repo this week and use it to build a simple background agent that auto-categorizes incoming news for your AI-ingest app.

items.title: tokencost: Optimize and reduce Claude API costs by testing thinking parameters
items.why_it_matters: A utility tool designed to help you benchmark and reduce Claude API costs by tweaking effort and thinking parameters. You can integrate this tool into your AI-ingest backend this week to track and slash your API spend while testing new features.
